subject: session date
fact: The session date is March 22nd, 2026.
source_quote: Date: Mar 22nd, 2026

subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra is a demon-haunted city.
source_quote: demon-haunted city of Maerimydra

subject: Spire of Inevitable Reckoning
fact: The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning contains the Lexicon of Oaths in its upper vaults.
source_quote: upper vaults where the Lexicon of Oaths was hidden

subject: ritual courtyard
fact: The ritual courtyard is paved with polished obsidian and features ebony pillars branded with the crimson six-fingered hand of Graz'zt.
source_quote: ritual courtyard of polished obsidian, its ebony pillars each branded with the crimson six-fingered hand of the demon lord Graz'zt

subject: Graz'zt
fact: Graz'zt is a demon lord whose symbol is a crimson six-fingered hand.
source_quote: crimson six-fingered hand of the demon lord Graz'zt

subject: Brandyr
fact: Brandyr is a fire giant who served as a sentinel over the ritual courtyard.
source_quote: fire giant Brandyr, a towering sentinel standing watch over a ritual courtyard

subject: Brandyr subdued
fact: Felkur cast an enchantment that dropped Brandyr into a deep slumber.
source_quote: Felkur unleashed a devastating enchantment that dropped the massive giant into a deep, helpless slumber

subject: Brandyr shrunk
fact: Bramgrim and Daein shrank Brandyr's frame to a fraction of her original size.
source_quote: Bramgrim and Daein worked in tandem to shrink her enormous frame down to a fraction of her original size

subject: Brandyr concealed
fact: Akritas shoved the shrunken Brandyr into a portable hole fitted with a breathing apparatus.
source_quote: Akritas — cloaked in invisibility — swept in and shoved the shrunken giant into a portable hole fitted with a breathing apparatus

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap is an imp companion who revealed the layout of the Spire and the nature of the evening's gathering.
source_quote: imp companion Pipyap, who — after some firm persuasion — revealed the layout of the Spire and the nature of the evening's gathering

subject: Vraxen
fact: Vraxen is a fox-faced Arcanaloth who serves as Graz'zt's contract enforcer.
source_quote: Vraxen, the fox-faced Arcanaloth who served as Graz'zt's contract enforcer

subject: Veperyd
fact: Veperyd is a smoke-weeping fire giant who runs the city's Colosseum, duels, and betting pool.
source_quote: Veperyd, a smoke-weeping fire giant who ran the city's Colosseum, duels, and betting pool

subject: Veris
fact: Veris is a cold and calculating mage from Mulmaster who serves as High Inquisitor.
source_quote: High Inquisitor Veris, a cold and calculating mage from Mulmaster

subject: runic key
fact: The runic key is kept by Vraxen and is the only means of accessing the upper vaults.
source_quote: Vraxen kept a runic key on his person — the only means of accessing the upper vaults

subject: Lexicon of Oaths
fact: The Lexicon of Oaths is hidden in the upper vaults of the Spire.
source_quote: upper vaults where the Lexicon of Oaths was hidden

subject: Felkur's identity
fact: The Lexicon of Oaths likely contains proof of Felkur's identity as the infamous Phantom Ghost thief of Mulmaster.
source_quote: the Lexicon itself likely contained proof of Felkur's identity as the infamous Phantom Ghost thief of Mulmaster

subject: Maram il Vass
fact: Maram il Vass engineered a double cross involving the Lexicon of Oaths.
source_quote: Maram il Vass had engineered a double cross

subject: party infiltration
fact: The party presented themselves as traders from Hillsfar to gain entry to the Spire.
source_quote: presenting themselves as traders from Hillsfar bearing critical information

subject: dining hall
fact: The dining hall contains a magically distorted table that makes a fire giant, a demon, and a human mage appear to be seated at eye level.
source_quote: magically distorted table made a fire giant, a demon, and a human mage all appear to be seated at eye level with one another

subject: Graz'zt illusion
fact: Daein conjured a flawless imitation of Graz'zt's voice to make the cambions believe the demon lord had smited Brandyr.
source_quote: Daein conjured a flawless imitation of Graz'zt's terrible voice, letting it boom through the courtyard with divine displeasure directed squarely at the fire giants

subject: cambions abandon posts
fact: The cambions abandoned their posts and took to the air to spread the news of the smiting.
source_quote: they abandoned their posts entirely and took to the air, spreading the glorious revelation to every demon in the city

subject: cambion guards
fact: The cambion guards were left bewildered by the sudden disappearance of their giant commander.
source_quote: The cambion guards stationed at the courtyard were left utterly bewildered, murmuring among themselves about the sudden disappearance of their giant commander

subject: owlbear chaos
fact: The party's owlbear leapt onto the formal dinner table and began smashing everything in sight.
source_quote: their owlbear companion — apparently concluding that since everyone at the table was pounding and roaring, this must simply be a pounding room — leapt onto the formal dinner table and began smashing everything in sight

subject: runic key stolen
fact: Daein used telekinesis to silently drag the runic key across the obsidian table into the party's possession.
source_quote: Daein reached out with his telekinetic power and silently dragged the runic key across the obsidian table and into the party's possession

subject: Veris framed
fact: Daein conjured an illusion of Veris's voice insinuating he was conspiring with Veperyd to skim from the betting pool.
source_quote: Daein conjured one final illusion — the voice of High Inquisitor Veris himself — insinuating that the mage had been conspiring with Veperyd to skim from the betting pool

subject: three-way brawl
fact: A three-way brawl erupted between demons, giants, and wizards after Vraxen ordered his guards to flay Veperyd.
source_quote: Vraxen shrieked about treason and ordered his guards to flay the giant, while Veperyd roared back with a promise to repay the debt in blood

subject: party escapes to vaults
fact: The party slipped away into the chaos and began their ascent toward the upper vaults of the Spire.
source_quote: The party slipped away into the chaos and began their ascent toward the upper vaults of the Spire

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap vanished somewhere in the chaos of the dinner and is no longer with the party.
source_quote: Pipyap was gone. The little imp, who had guided them, bargained with them, and eaten their food with such relish, had simply vanished somewhere in the chaos of the dinner

subject: teleportation device
fact: A teleportation device that would carry the party out of Maerimydra is waiting somewhere above.
source_quote: The Lexicon of Oaths and the teleportation device that would carry them out of Maerimydra still waited somewhere above

subject: session date
fact: The session covers memorable moments.
source_quote: Date: Memorable Moments

subject: performance roll
fact: The party achieved a natural 20 on a performance roll.
source_quote: After a natural 20 performance roll

subject: manhunt avoidance
fact: The party bypassed a potential city-wide manhunt by exploiting the Cambions' smug resentment of the fire giants.
source_quote: the party completely bypassed a potential city-wide manhunt by exploiting the Cambions' smug resentment of the fire giants

subject: Felkur
fact: Felkur deduces that Maram il Vass has engineered a double cross.
source_quote: Felkur deduces that Maram il Vass has engineered a double cross

subject: Lexicon of Oaths
fact: The Lexicon of Oaths likely contains proof of Felkur's identity as the Phantom Ghost.
source_quote: the Lexicon of Oaths likely contains proof of Felkur's identity as the Phantom Ghost

subject: High Inquisitor Veris
fact: The party must reach the Lexicon of Oaths before High Inquisitor Veris does.
source_quote: the party must reach it before High Inquisitor Veris does

subject: insight rolls
fact: A chain of insight rolls at the dinner planning stage revealed the full scope of the trap laid by Maram il Vass.
source_quote: A chain of insight rolls at the dinner planning stage revealed the full scope of the trap laid by Maram il Vass

subject: Owlbear
fact: The party's Owlbear companion jumps onto the formal dinner table.
source_quote: The party's Owlbear companion jumps onto the formal dinner table

subject: Owlbear destruction
fact: The Owlbear begins roaring and smashing things at the table.
source_quote: begins roaring and smashing things

subject: Owlbear motivation
fact: The Owlbear concludes that since everyone else was pounding the table, this must simply be a pounding room.
source_quote: apparently concluding that since everyone else was pounding the table, this must simply be a pounding room

subject: key theft cover
fact: The Owlbear's chaotic intervention provided the perfect cover for Daein to telekinetically steal Vraxen's runic key.
source_quote: The Owlbear's chaotic intervention provided the perfect cover for Daein to telekinetically steal Vraxen's runic key

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap the imp is no longer with the party as they slip away from the dinner table battle.
source_quote: they notice that Pipyap the imp is no longer with them

subject: Pipyap disappearance
fact: The mysterious disappearance of their imp informant mid-chaos raises unsettling questions about his loyalties and whereabouts.
source_quote: The mysterious disappearance of their imp informant mid-chaos raises unsettling questions about his loyalties and whereabouts

subject: Maerimydra
fact: The party coordinates a complex plan to kidnap a fire giant named Brandyr to use as a pawn in their infiltration of the Spire of Inevitable Reckoning.
source_quote: The party coordinates a complex plan to kidnap a fire giant named Brandyr to use as a pawn in their infiltration of the Spire of Inevitable Reckoning

subject: Brandyr
fact: The party's objective is to capture Brandyr, shrink her, and hide her in a portable hole.
source_quote: to capture Brandyr, shrink her, and hide her in a portable hole

subject: spell preparation
fact: Felkur prepares to cast the sleep spell while Bramgrim and Daein ready the reduction scrolls.
source_quote: Felkur prepares to cast the sleep spell while Bramgrim and Daein ready the reduction scrolls

subject: Akritas
fact: Akritas prepares to move invisibly under the cover of a stealth-enhancing aura to secure the giant once she is incapacitated.
source_quote: Akritas prepares to move invisibly under the cover of a stealth-enhancing aura to secure the giant once she is incapacitated

subject: Obsidian Courtyard
fact: The party positions themselves around a courtyard with a polished obsidian floor and ebony pillars emblazoned with six-fingered hands.
source_quote: The party positions themselves around a courtyard with a polished obsidian floor and ebony pillars emblazoned with six-fingered hands

subject: Brandyr incapacitation
fact: Felkur casts a powerful sleep spell using a magical token, successfully rendering the fire giant Brandyr incapacitated.
source_quote: Felkur casts a powerful sleep spell using a magical token, successfully rendering the fire giant Brandyr incapacitated

subject: Akritas
fact: Akritas uses Nature's Veil to become invisible and approaches the sleeping giant.
source_quote: Akritas uses Nature's Veil to become invisible and approaches the sleeping giant

subject: Brandyr reduction
fact: Bramgrim and Daein both cast reduction spells to shrink the giant's massive frame down to a manageable scale.
source_quote: Bramgrim and Daein both cast reduction spells to shrink the giant's massive frame down to a manageable scale

subject: Brandyr containment
fact: Akritas shoves the shrunken, unconscious giant into a portable hole equipped with a breathing apparatus.
source_quote: Akritas shoves the shrunken, unconscious giant into a portable hole equipped with a breathing apparatus

subject: courtyard retreat
fact: The party uses the confusion of the moment and magical stealth to begin a quiet retreat from the courtyard.
source_quote: The party uses the confusion of the moment and magical stealth to begin a quiet retreat from the courtyard

subject: Cambion guards
fact: Several cambion guards are left bewildered by the giant's sudden disappearance, debating among themselves whether Graz'zt personally intervened.
source_quote: Several cambion guards are left bewildered by the giant's sudden disappearance, debating among themselves whether Graz'zt personally intervened

subject: Pass Without Trace
fact: Akritas casts Pass Without Trace to mask the party's movements and allow them to move together unseen.
source_quote: Akritas casts Pass Without Trace to mask the party's movements and allow them to move together unseen

subject: Graz'zt illusion
fact: Daein creates a magical illusion mimicking the voice of the demon lord Graz'zt, expressing divine displeasure with the fire giants.
source_quote: Daein creates a magical illusion mimicking the voice of the demon lord Graz'zt, expressing divine displeasure with the fire giants

subject: illusion effectiveness
fact: The illusion perfectly plays into the Cambions' existing prejudices against the fire giants.
source_quote: The illusion perfectly plays into the Cambions' existing prejudices against the fire giants

subject: Cambion reverence
fact: The Cambions, convinced that Graz'zt personally removed the fire giant, fall to their knees in reverence.
source_quote: The Cambions, convinced that Graz'zt personally removed the fire giant, fall to their knees in reverence

subject: guard abandonment
fact: The demonic guards abandon their posts to spread the news of the fire giants' fall from grace throughout the city, clearing a path for the party.
source_quote: The demonic guards abandon their posts to spread the news of the fire giants' fall from grace throughout the city, clearing a path for the party

subject: deception advantage
fact: The party is granted advantage on upcoming deception, intimidation, or persuasion checks against Vraxen and Veperyd as a result of the spreading rumor.
source_quote: The party is granted advantage on upcoming deception, intimidation, or persuasion checks against Vraxen and Veperyd as a result of the spreading rumor

subject: Pipyap
fact: The party questions their imp companion about the layout of Vraxen's tower and how to infiltrate the upcoming dinner.
source_quote: The party questions their imp companion about the layout of Vraxen's tower and how to infiltrate the upcoming dinner

subject: dining hall
fact: Pipyap describes the dining hall and a magical table that adjusts the perceived size of guests so everyone appears equal.
source_quote: Pipyap describes the dining hall and a magical table that adjusts the perceived size of guests so everyone appears equal

subject: Pipyap intimidation
fact: Daein intimidates Pipyap into providing more information without demanding more food as payment.
source_quote: Daein intimidates Pipyap into providing more information without demanding more food as payment

subject: runic key
fact: Pipyap reveals that Vraxen carries a runic key necessary to reach the upper vaults where the Lexicon of Oaths is kept.
source_quote: Pipyap reveals that Vraxen carries a runic key necessary to reach the upper vaults where the Lexicon of Oaths is kept

subject: Felkur
fact: Felkur deduces through insight that Maram il Vass has set up a double cross.
source_quote: Felkur deduces through insight that Maram il Vass has set up a double cross

fact: The party decides to pose as traders from Hillsfar with information about the 'Phantom Ghost' thief to gain entry to the dinner.
source_quote: The party decides to pose as traders from Hillsfar with information about the 'Phantom Ghost' thief to gain entry to the dinner

subject: Spire of Inevitable Reckoning
fact: The party bluffs their way past the front guards of the Spire of Inevitable Reckoning and enters the dining hall.
source_quote: The party bluffs their way past the front guards of the Spire of Inevitable Reckoning and enters the dining hall

subject: guard dismissal
fact: The party approaches the guards, who initially dismiss them as slaves.
source_quote: The party approaches the guards, who initially dismiss them as slaves

subject: trader persuasion
fact: Bramgrim and Daein persuade the guards that they are traders carrying critical information for Vraxen.
source_quote: Bramgrim and Daein persuade the guards that they are traders carrying critical information for Vraxen

subject: spire entry
fact: The guards, impressed by their boldness, allow the party to enter the spire to attend the dinner.
source_quote: The guards, impressed by their boldness, allow the party to enter the spire to attend the dinner

subject: dining hall
fact: The party enters the dining hall and observes a magical table that distorts the sizes of the guests so they appear to be at face level with one another.
source_quote: The party enters the dining hall and observes a magical table that distorts the sizes of the guests so they appear to be at face level with one another

fact: Vraxen is present at the dinner table.

fact: Veperyd is present at the dinner table.

fact: High Inquisitor Veris is present at the dinner table.

fact: Vraxen accused Veperyd of skimming from the betting pool.

fact: Veperyd reacted violently by slamming the table and claiming the Abyss demands sacrifice.

fact: High Inquisitor Veris attempted to interrogate Felkur.
source_quote: High Inquisitor Veris attempts to interrogate Felkur

fact: High Inquisitor Veris identified Felkur as a master smith with ties to Mulmaster.

fact: Daein and Bramgrim successfully redirected the conversation to fuel paranoia between Vraxen and Veperyd.

fact: The Six-Fingered Courtyard is a ritual area on a plateau in Maerimydra featuring a polished obsidian floor surrounded by ebony pillars.

fact: Maerimydra is a city in the Underdark currently occupied by fire giants who have made a pact with the demon lord Graz'zt.
